label: 1776 CE
era_summary: 1776 (MDCCLXXVI) was a leap year starting on Monday of the Gregorian calendar and a leap year starting on Friday of the Julian calendar, the 1776th year of the Common Era (CE) and Anno Domini (AD) designations, the 776th year of the 2nd millennium, the 76th year of the 18th century, and the 7th year of the 1770s decade. As of the start of 1776, the Gregorian calendar was 11 days ahead of the Julian calendar, which remained in localized use until 1923.

rome_italy.summary: Brecke records no major conflicts touching this region in 1776 (safety scored at the era baseline of 85). No Maddison GDP coverage for this region — economy held at neutral 50, reflecting the thin pre-modern record outside the European core. Governance held at neutral (V-Dem coverage starts 1789).

andalusia_iberia.summary: Active conflicts in 1776: Portugal-Mbailundu, Ndulu, Bihe (Angola), 1774-76; Spain-Portugal (South America), 1776-77. Maddison records GDP/capita 1929 (PRT, 1776) — used as the economy proxy for the whole region. Governance held at neutral (V-Dem coverage starts 1789).
